pairs.question: What was the full name of the predecessor company to AK Steel?
pairs.answer: The predecessor company to AK Steel was Armco.

pairs.question: In which year was AK Steel acquired by Cleveland-Cliffs?
pairs.answer: AK Steel was acquired by Cleveland-Cliffs in 2020.

pairs.question: List the locations of AK Steel's steel plants and tube manufacturing plants.
pairs.answer: AK Steel operated steel plants and tube manufacturing plants in Ashland (Kentucky), Butler (Pennsylvania), Coshocton (Ohio), Dearborn (Michigan), Mansfield (Ohio), Middletown (Ohio), Rockport (Indiana), and Zanesville (Ohio).

pairs.question: What types of steel products did AK Steel produce, and which industries were the primary customers?
pairs.answer: AK Steel produced flat-rolled carbon, stainless, and electrical steel products, as well as carbon and stainless steel tubing. The primary customers were the automotive industry (63% of 2018 sales), infrastructure and manufacturing (15%), and distributors and converters (22%).

pairs.question: What awards did AK Steel receive in 2019?
pairs.answer: In 2019, AK Steel was named GM Supplier of the Year for Non Fabricated Steel by General Motors and received the Smart Pillar Award from Ford at the 21st annual Ford World Excellence Awards.

pairs.question: What criticisms were leveled against AK Steel regarding its operations?
pairs.answer: AK Steel faced criticism for its record regarding pollution and worker safety.

pairs.question: Did AK Steel have manufacturing operations outside the United States? If so, where?
pairs.answer: Yes, AK Steel had manufacturing operations in Canada, Mexico, and Western Europe.
